behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md explicitly names all required strings, variable names (chart_form_data, _build_update_payload, _find_chart), and literal error messages that tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction dictates exact code tokens like `utils_json.loads(chart_params)`, `form_data=chart_form_data`, `identifier.isdigit()`, `int(identifier)`, `id_column="uuid"`, `return {"slice_name": request.chart_name}`, and `ChartConfig | None = Field(None, ...)` — essentially prescribing the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific helper function names (`_build_update_payload`, `_find_chart`), specific variable names (`chart_form_data`), and exact literal strings like `return {"slice_name": request.chart_name}` — alternative correct fixes using different names would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most fail_to_pass tests are `open(path).read(); assertIn(...)` substring checks (e.g., TestSerializeChartFormData, TestBuildUpdatePayloadHelper, TestFindChartHelper, TestErrorResponseStructure) — only ruff subprocess tests execute code.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests check specific strings that wouldn't appear unless implementations are made; stubs returning None cannot produce these literal substrings in source.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four bugs described are covered by at least one test class.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are organized into named TestCase classes per bug/behavior with descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code bug-fixing, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers appear consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names exact helper function names `_build_update_payload()` and `_find_chart()` and exact variable `chart_form_data`, leaking localization and naming decisions.
